steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure required
steps.content: The sentence uses a cleft construction: "[noun] is what has ______." The blank follows the auxiliary "has," so it requires a past participle followed by its objects in standard verb order (verb $\Rightarrow$ indirect object $\Rightarrow$ direct object). The sentence is declarative, so it must end with a period.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
Evaluate each answer choice against those requirements:
  • Choice A — ‘earned him his enduring fame?’: The verb order is correct, but it ends with a question mark, which is wrong for a declarative statement. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘his enduring fame earned him?’: It ends with a question mark and places the object before the verb, violating both the declarative and cleft-construction requirements. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘his enduring fame earned him.’: It ends correctly with a period, but places the direct object before the verb, disrupting the required cleft construction word order. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘earned him his enduring fame.’: The past participle "earned" comes first, followed by indirect object "him" and direct object "his enduring fame," and the sentence closes with a period. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
